rxn_class_summary: electroreductive three-component dicarbofunctionalization of styrenes with aryl iodides and unactivated alkyl iodides via Ni catalysis
electrochemical_role: cathodic reduction of Ni(II) precatalyst to Ni(0) initiates the cycle; Ni(II) species (G) generated after alkyl iodide activation is re-reduced cathodically to Ni(0); sacrificial Fe anode provides counter-oxidation (M to M^n+)
electron_flow: Cathode reduces Ni(II) to Ni(0) (A), which undergoes oxidative addition with ArI to give Ar-Ni(II) (B); Ni(I) (F) abstracts iodide from alkyl iodide to give alkyl radical (C) and Ni(II) (G); the alkyl radical adds to styrene forming benzylic radical (D) which combines with Ar-Ni(II) (B) to form Ni(III) (E); reductive elimination delivers the product and Ni(I) (F), while Ni(II) (G) is re-reduced at the cathode to Ni(0).

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Multicomponent product 4a abolished (0%); TEMPO-alkyl adduct 7 isolated in 13% from full system and 31% from alkyl iodide 3a alone, confirming alkyl radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: DMPO (5,5-dimethyl-1-pyrroline N-oxide)
probe_reagents.observation: EPR spin-adduct observed (g = 2.00502, αN = 14.54 G, αH = 20.92 G) for 3a, 1a+3a, and 1a+2a+3a; 1a and 2a alone were EPR-inactive, confirming alkyl radical originates from alkyl iodide 3a

notes: Tertiary alkyl bromides gave higher yields than tertiary alkyl iodides (63% vs 13%), suggesting halide identity influences radical generation efficiency. The alkyl radical adds preferentially to styrene to give the thermodynamically favored benzylic radical, which dictates regioselectivity of the two C-C bond formations (Csp3-Csp3 and Csp3-Csp2).
